entry.title: Inside SAP: Discover How SAP's IT Drives Transformation and Innovation
entry.content:
The Discover SAP YouTube Series continues with our next speaker, Cloud Solution Architect in Platform Center of Excellence, Alexandra Hofmann.
See how we use the latest SAP Business Technology Platform technologies, AI capabilities, and agile methodologies to optimize operations, increase developer efficiency, and accelerate innovation as a large-scale enterprise.

Registration link: https://events.sap.com/2024-1231-sap-btp-discovery-jouney-webinar-global/en/home

Join the session: https://www.youtube.com/live/nZWnZh-adb4

entry.title: Planning calender for making periodic deliveries for raw materials
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am setting the periodic delivery of raw materials :- first week wednesday in every month. What I did is

1. changing the lot size procedure to a custom procedure. It has priod lot sizing procedure(P) , indicator as as in PPC calender (K) and scheduling period start = availability date    ( see the screenshot) 

2. created a new calender with 'monthly ' as calculation rule and with periods as in screenshots

my questions are 

a. is my period creation wrong ? If so what should i change?

b. have I missed any other settings in material master ? My MRP type is PD and I assigned the planning calender and Lot sizing procedure to the material master already. 

c. What is the steps if I need a period delivery and the MRP run based on a planning calender?

Thank you. Please feel free to ask questions if I am not clear with any information.

Best regards,

Kailas

entry.title: People Analytics - Deprecation of classic design experience
entry.content:
Hello Everyone, 

as you probably noticed - Classic Design Experience in People Analytics Story will be deprecated and customers have to convert their reports created in CDE to Optimized Design Experience. 

One of limitations of Optimized Design Experience is that it doesn't support blending data in tables/charts. 

Many customers use the blending data feature in their stories. And after conversion from CDE to ODE the tables/chars that used blending data become useful. 

So the questions are: Is there any workaround for that? Is SAP planning to enable the blending data feature in Optimized Design Experience before deprecation of Classic Design Experience?


Best regards

Lukasz

entry.title: Other CDS View for A_EquipmentPartner
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am looking for a CDS view that connects the Equipment to the related BusinessPartnerRoles like A_EquipmentPartner. Does anyone have a CDS view that can be used for Data Extraction?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Maintenance Plan Call Dates called earlier than Call Horizon
entry.content:
Hi,

May I ask for help in explaining why the Call Date for the first Call Object line was called when there is a Call Horizon of 45 DAYS? The details of the Maintenance Plan are as follows:

Maintenance Plan created and scheduled : 29.01.2024

Cycle Unit : 1000 HR

Call Horizon : 45 DAYS

Start Counter Reading : 75830 (counter reading when Maintenance Plan was created)

First Planned Date : 28.05.2024

First Call Date : 17.03.2024

Scheduling indicator : 3 - Performance Based

 

What is the basis of the Call Date? Shouldn't it be based on the Call Horizon? Thank you

entry.title: Re: In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA – Ledger Scoping Q&A
entry.content:
Hi Srinivasa,

I am not aware of any functional differences between SAP S/4HANA (on-premise) and SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition. 

Just note that there are considerable differences between these and the SAP S/4HANA Cloud offering we are talking about in this blog post and in the meeting series.

Best regards,

Verena

entry.title: Re: UI5 Vscode: Adaptation project for object page (How t...
entry.content: On my side, I got the issue. on ui5.yaml, i corrected "ignoreCertErrors" by "IgnoreCertError" (without s). I had another configuration file on another project. Now I can run the preview mode correctly.

entry.title: Adding Custom Metrics to a CAP application using cap-js/telementry plugin and SAP Cloud Logging
entry.content:
About

Observability helps to take data-driven informed decisions based on runtime Observability telemetry data (logs, metrics, traces, events, ...) and provides insights into SAP & customer services and systems to understand and improve efficiency, scalability, resilience, and availability.

When you seek for fully detailed observability data of your application, you can use SAP Cloud Logging. SAP Cloud Logging allows you to slice and dice logs, metrics, and traces in order to analyze functional and performance behaviour of your BTP workload on Cloud Foundry and Kyma.

Metrics are "measurements captured at runtime", which help you understand your app's health and performance.

Out of the box, @cap-js/telemetry offers metrics regarding the app's database pool, namely the pool info statistics of generic-pool.

@cap-js/telemetry is a CDS plugin providing observability features, including automatic OpenTelemetry instrumentation.

Documentation can be found at cap.cloud.sap and opentelemetry.io.

Prerequisite:

1. You have Incident Management Application configured till this step

Setup Metrics collection

Metrics are "measurements captured at runtime", which help you understand your app's health and performance.

Out of the box, @cap-js/telemetry offers metrics regarding the app's database pool, namely the pool info statistics of generic-pool.

Run and test locally

Once the application has all the requried configurations. The application can be tested locally.

  1. In the terminal run the application with cds watch

  2. Open the application and create a incident.

  3. Check the logs in terminal

    Example db pool metrics printed to the console:

    [telemetry] - db.pool:
    size | available | pending
      1/1 |       1/1 |       0
     

Adding custom metrics to the application

The business user could check how many "High" incidents are being currently handled in the system using the custom metrics. Custom metrics can be added as shown in the following example:

  1. Create a new file at root of the application, name it server.js and paste the following code:
     const cds = require('@sap/cds')
 // Import necessary modules
 const { metrics } = require('@opentelemetry/api');

 const meter = metrics.getMeter('@capire/incidents:incidents.urgency.high');
 // Create a counter
 const counter = meter.createUpDownCounter('incidents.urgency.high');

 cds.on('served', async () => {
 const { ProcessorService } = cds.services
 // Increase count when new incident with high urgency is created
 ProcessorService.after("CREATE", "Incidents", (results, req) => {
     if (results.urgency_code === "H" && results.status_code !== "C") {
     counter.add(1, { 'sap.tenancy.tenant_id': req.tenant });
     }
 });
 // Reduce count once incident is closed
 ProcessorService.after("UPDATE", "Incidents", (results, req) => {
     if (results.urgency_code === "H" && results?.status_code === "C") {
     counter.add(-1, { 'sap.tenancy.tenant_id': req.tenant });
     }
 });
 });
 module.exports = cds.server

Deploy the application

  • For BTP Cloud Foundry runtime, follow the steps described here
  • For BTP Kyma runtime, follow the steps described here

Access Application Metrics

  1. On the left side of the screen, click the breadcrumb and choose Discover.
  2. On the Discover Screen, logs-cfsyslog-* is selected by default. You can see the application logs.
  3. Choose logs-cfsyslog-*, and dropdown menu appears. Select metrics-otel-v1-*. This will display the metrics from the application.
  4. Select the fields from the left side of the screen to view specific fields. The output should be displayed like this:

Create Visualisation for Custom Metrics

In the previous chapter, you created a custom metric to count the "High" urgency incidents. You can create a custom visualisation to show the count.

  1. On the left side breadcrumb, choose Visualize.

  2. Choose Create visualization.

  3. A dialog appears. Choose Metric.

  4. On the right side of the screen, choose Metric.

  5. Choose the dropdown under Aggregation and select Top Hit.

  6. Choose the Field dropdown menu and select the value.

  7. Under the Custom label, enter a name such as High Urgency Incidents.

  8. Choose Update at the bottom of the screen.

  9. The visualisation will be created. 

     

     

     

    You can save this visualisation for quick access.

 #Observability #opentelemetry #SAPCloudLogging #SAPBTP #BTP #Metrics

entry.title: Re: Search help assignment for field is not inherited fro...
entry.content: cast(Land1 as land1 preserving type) as Land1

entry.title: Why is a Code List based on Custom CDS View not working in Display/Change Maintenance Order Page?
entry.content:
I have created and added a Custom Field that is of type Code List based on CDS View.

The Primary Data source for this Custom CDS View is based off a Custom Business Object.

The Scenario of this Custom CDS View is "Value Help". 

I have successfully published all the above components, however upon adding the Custom Field in the Maintenance Order Page (Display/ Change) thru Adapt UI, it remains to display as a Text Field instead of an option to open the Code List.

entry.title: EWM Performance issue due to growth of table /SCWM/STOCK_IW02
entry.content:
Hello EWM Experts,

In production environment, we noticed abnormal growth of SAP table /SCWM/STOCK_IW02, and found this SAP note below regarding it:

2019324 - General performance issues in EWM

We then tried to apply this note / tool in our quality environment, by scheduling this report /LIME/BACKGROUND_DELETE_EXEC as recommended in the note, to run daily in a rather busy system we have.

In most of the days when the job is running, we noticed related SM13 error as following:

Function Module        /SCWM/GM_UPDATE

Status                 Update was canceled

Report                 /SCWM/LHU_TO_UPDF23

Row                    310

Error details   Class:     /LIME/CORE Number:    134
Object is already locked: /LIME/E_RESERVE; parameters: /LIME/E_RESERVE
 /LIME/E_RESERVE /LIME/E_RESERVE

 

Therefore, we can't use this in our productive environment. 

We would like to know if anybody also faced this increase of /SCWM/STOCK_IW02 table and how do you resolve it? 

Thanks in advance!

Best Regards, 
Shiv

entry.content: Hi @MinhTriLe, did you ended coding your own to extract?

entry.content:
Hi,

is it possible to create any rule to delete some group of users from Employee Profile when user is not active, after 6 months system should delete all history about this user in EP, LMS and IAS.

Thank you in advance!

Regards,

Aneta

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Employee Profile

entry.title: Re: Error in Build process automation
entry.content: This was the correct reply, and I was able to correct the issue with the design console. Thanks @PatrikMajer for your help!

entry.title: Re: How to use fuzzy search in cds query
entry.content: To my opinion there should be no issue using OR or AND. If AND returns values OR should at least also return data (less strict). Maybe there is a syntax issue. I should try it again.

entry.title: Proper URL for access to SAC from an external platform
entry.content:
Dear all.

I am trying to create a proper URL for access to a SAC report from an external platform, i.e. Sales Force. The  SAC report is based on an Bex query. The Bex query has only one mandatory, input-ready variable GCO_TIME_REF (manual, not Customer Exit), which is based on a characteristics of type Date (0DATE).

The URL is as of the following:

https://xxx.eu1.sapanalytics.cloud/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/011/app.html#/story2&/s2/9FC813066083DC87D2B1D7EEBA7B2DBD/?v01Model=Cev4161j0g62i4beei83f1t183v&v01Par=GCO_TIME_REF&v01Val=20240625&mode=view

Due to data security puposes, I have replaced the first part of the URL, which refers to the tenant, with xxx. 

Somehow the URL doesn't work. When ever i tried to open it, i just landed on the popup windows, where i have to insert the value of the variable GCO_TIME_REF manually. The date "20240625" of the URL is somehow not accepted.

Can someone please tell me why?

Many thanks in advance

BR

entry.title: Re: Blocking of Sales Order thru SP Transaction Notification
entry.content:
Hi,

try this

 

IF(@object_type = '17') and (@transaction_type in ('A', 'U'))

BEGIN

IF Exists (SELECT T0.CardCode FROM ORDR T0 JOIN OINV T1 ON T0.CardCode=T1.CardCode

WHERE T0.DocEntry = _of_cols_val_tab_del AND T1.[DocStatus] = 'O' AND T1.CANCELED='N' )

BEGIN

SET  = 1001

SET _message = 'You have Pending AR invoice Open, Please Settle Customer Account. Thank you!'

End

End

 

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan Call Dates called earlier than Call...
entry.content:
Greetings,

as per documentation:

So according to your maintenance plan a call object should be created 45 days before the cycle 1000 hours is reached. The estimated date when cycle is reached is calculated based on counter annual estimate and regular measurement documents input.

You can also try clicking on the call algorithm button in IP10. System should show how it calculated call number data.

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Issues with Job Requisition Creation and Recruiter Assignment in Position EC
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We have a customer requirement where the hiring manager creates a job requisition from Position EC. Our process involves:

  1. Hiring managers create job requisitions from Position EC without an assigned recruiter.
  2. Customer requests to route the requisition to a recruiting team group for an available recruiter to self-assign to assist the manager in completing the job requisition and proceeding with the approvals. 
  3. The route map designed for job requisition was initiated as a collaborative step between the hiring manager and the recruiter and then routed to the HR manager.

I've set the recruiter as a collaborative step in the draft and modify stage of the route map. However, the recruiter field is empty at creation, causing permission issues. Also, recruiting team users can view unapproved jobs but can't edit them until approval, so the job requisition will be stuck at the first stage.

Any advice or thoughts would be highly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan Call Dates called earlier than Call...
entry.content: Thank you Matjaz. I have checked the algorithm button and I have the data below

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan Call Dates called earlier than Call...
entry.content: Annual Estimate = 2367.52 Activity per day = 6.49 Start Counter Reading = 75830 Next Counter Reading = 76830 The first object was called at 17.03.2024 which is not 45 days from the Planned Date of 28.05.2024. My question is why was the Maintenance Order called at 17.03.2024? Thank you.

entry.content:

The reason is to get the corresponding reporting capability access on learning.sap.com.


Best Regards,
Florian

entry.title: Can we re-purpose an existing company code's bank account for multiple company codes?
entry.content:
Hi,

 

We have a functional bank account with MBC connectivity and receive statements for the existing company code's transactions regularly. We now intend to use the same bank account and extend this to newer company codes. Is this allowed in system? What are the potential drawbacks of having this setup? While we checked few documentations which says this can be done by converting error message to warning, we would like to understand the impact of setting this up in terms of manual intervention required for bank statements on the new company codes/EBS setup issue, etc.

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Pooja

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Continuous Influence - Results of 06/2024 Review Cycle
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud has completed the third review of Improvement Requests since opening the SAP Continuous Influence sessions in Q4, 2022. To date, we have received 168 Improvement Requests and 862 votes across all session. We highly appreciate the active participation from customers and partners who contribute. Your requests, detailed description of use cases, and votes gives us an unique opportunity to connect with you at scale and use your input to prioritise and scope deliverables.

We have evaluated the top voted requests and also some requests with lower votes that we frequently hear through other feedback channels, such as Customer Technology Advisory Boards (CTABs), customer user groups, customer co-innovation, partner feedback and interactions with colleagues in the field, to mention just a few.

Below is a list of the 4 Continuous Influence Sessions for SAP Commerce Cloud with links to the results blog providing a list of requests based on the following status updates:

    • have been delivered (10) or are already offered (2)
    • have been moved forward in the product planning process (accepted (3) / planned long-term (5))
    • have not (yet) made it into product planning, since further evaluation may be required (for long-term consideration (3))
    • were not accepted for planning (not planned (3))

 

Session Name & LinkDescriptionResults Blog
SAP Commerce Cloud AutomationFor technical users operating SAP Commerce Cloud instanceshttps://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3497 
SAP Commerce Cloud Developer ExperienceFor technical users customising SAP Commerce Cloudhttps://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3500
SAP Commerce Cloud Pre-built IntegrationsFor technical users integrating SAP Commerce Cloud with other SAP solutionshttps://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3501
SAP Commerce Cloud Business FeaturesFor business (and technical) users with requests related to business capabilities of SAP Commerce Cloudhttps://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/blog/3502 


Below is a list of blog posts with results from former Review Cycles for SAP Commerce Cloud Continuous Influence:

    • Results of 11/2023 Review Cycle
    • Results of 06/2023 Review Cycle

Please see this blog post if you have not yet registered for and accessed the SAP Commerce Cloud Influence Sessions and would like to learn more.

We look forward to hearing more from and from more of you in the future!
​

entry.title: Re: How to Set Up the Email Notification for Flexible Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Yes @karthikeyansermaiya, this works in an on-premise system as well. These email templates are nice for informing about the outcome of an approval process. In some cases you would perhaps want to notify only about rejections, for instance in processes where the normal and expected outcome is an approval.

Some flexible workflow scenarios also have a deadline notification email template (e.g. purchase requisition approval and purchase order approval). It can be used to notify the recipient of the workitem. In my opinion those should only be used to notify about an approaching (or passed) deadline, while the extended notification for business workflow solution is a better solution for email notifications about work items waiting to be processed. Unfortunately we haven't set it up here yet, so at the moment I am not sure how well it will work with flexible workflows.

entry.title: LOAD_PROGRAM_TABLE_MISMATCH error
entry.content:
The "load_program_table_mismatch" error in SAP usually means there's a problem with the versions of some program files not matching up correctly. Here's a simple explanation of why it happens and how to fix it:

Why it happens:
System Update: If your SAP system was recently updated or patched, it might cause some files to be out of sync.
Incomplete Changes: If some program changes weren't properly loaded or imported into the system, this mismatch can occur.
Temporary Issues: Sometimes, the system's temporary memory (called the buffer) might get messed up or outdated.

The "load_program_table_mismatch" error in SAP usually means there's a problem with the versions of some program files not matching up correctly. Here's a simple explanation of why it happens and how to fix it:

Why it happens:
System Update: If your SAP system was recently updated or patched, it might cause some files to be out of sync.
Incomplete Changes: If some program changes weren't properly loaded or imported into the system, this mismatch can occur.
Temporary Issues: Sometimes, the system's temporary memory (called the buffer) might get messed up or outdated.
How to fix it:
Refresh Memory: Clear the system's temporary memory to make it reload the correct versions of the programs.
Re-import Changes: Make sure all changes or updates to the system were fully and correctly applied.
Check for Updates: Look into any recent updates or patches to see if there are known issues or additional steps needed.
System Check: Run a system check to find and fix any inconsistencies.
Solution 1:

Issue: LOAD_PROGRAM_TABLE_MISMATCH

The error occurred because the SAP system was recently updated or patched, causing some files to be out of sync.

The error won't occur after the upgrade process was completed.

Solution 2:

In some cases you need to regenerate specific coding and tables.

Regenerate programs

Use program TOUCHSRC.

Use program name as TOUCHTAB or TOUCHALL and execute.

you'll get the confirmation as Program touched successfully.

entry.title: Re: Functional Location Edit Mask
entry.content:
Greetings,

you could define a generic edit mask but with this you lose the SAP standard hierarchy distribution - you would need to manually define the hierarchy when you create a functional location. 

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Steps to make SAP Lumira installation
entry.content:
Hello All,

Can you please mention as steps how I install SAP Lumira Designer on my desktop?

I ask you because I try too much , but I still I do not get it. 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Tuned Profile Settings for Adaptive Server Linux
entry.content:
I followed up on these two parameters. 
I am still waiting for some input from colleagues but it seems that these default parameter settings apply to Redhat 8.x (not sure whether all 8x release are affected or not). Redhat 9.x seems not to be affected - have been told these parameters don't exist in 9.x.  
We also discussed whether or not to put these settings in default SAP tune configuration. As your report of this issue is the only one we are aware of, we decided to not put it to some tune configuration for now. 
But if this turns out to be a widespread issue the topic will be re-discussed again. 
HTH 

Tilman

you could try creating two maintenance plans. One for 6th day in the month and one for 21st day in month. 

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Alternative Reconciliation Account for Customer
entry.content:
Hello,

We need to set up the functionality Alternative Reconciliation Account in SD processes. We have been analyzing all the changes that need to be done and it seems that we'll be blocked in the point where we need to assign the account determination procedure with the billing type. Could you please let us know how to configure this functionality in SAP Cloud?

We're following this on-prem related 3rd party documentation: https://onlyordinarysapnotes.com/2013/08/26/alternative-reconciliation-account-for-customer/

Thank you!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Alternative Reconciliation Account for Customer
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

With S/4HANA Cloud 2402.3, SAP released this functionality in the Cloud edition. Here you can find the documentation: SAP Help - Determination of G/L accounts for reconciliation and cash settlement 

The functionality is enabled via the Flexible Billing Document Posting configuration. It is the Usage Task DET_RECACC_CSHSET and further down in the document you will also see example implementation. (Set Account Determination Procedure for Reconciliation Accounts and Cash Settlement Accounts)

Please refer to 3487300 - Alternative Reconciliation Account Determination in SD - S/4HANA Cloud.

Best regards,

SAP Expert

entry.title: Hydrogen Convention in Essen - Shaping the Future of Energy
entry.content:
The energy transition towards a non-fossil driven economy is unquestionably the biggest global challenge we currently face. Among the most promising alternatives ranks the hydrogen economy, in which Green Hydrogen will substitute coal, oil or natural gas.

H2UB is Europe’s leading Open Innovation Platform for the whole hydrogen value chain and organizer of the Hydrogen Convention, a vibrant event for start-ups, investors, entrepreneurs, and companies driving the energy transition. Located in Essen, Germany, which is the headquarter for numerous energy companies such as OGE, E.ON and RWE, it has become one of the most profiled platforms to show and discuss the latest innovations, the progress as well as remaining obstacles and opportunities.

SAP´s ambition to support Energy Companies in their transition to renewables and sustainable energy production, has led to various partnerships and projects in the area of hydrogen. Topics such as tracing the chain of custody, mass balancing and, most importantly, carbon accounting to track the CO2-footprint along the value chain including credits and certificates are part of SAP´s sustainability solution portfolio.

Doors were opened on the 18th of June at 10:00 and the event centered around a few dozens of start-ups demonstrating numerous areas of innovations, spanning from materials management, new ways to produce ammonia to software for optimizing the production.

The starting point was a thought-provoking keynote, held by Michael Liebreich, CEO of EcoPragma, an investor dedicated to the net zero transition in energy. He sharply analyzed the opportunities and risks of the hydrogen economy and raised concerns about the competitiveness in various areas, calling for a more focused approach towards areas in which hydrogen will be a competitive source of energy. He pointed out that scalability, availability and efficiency in production and logistics remain obstacles.

In the following, various start-ups had the opportunity to introduce their offerings and the market model. Managers, researchers, and analysts joined panels to discuss facets of the transition, giving insights to projects such as the expansion of the H2-pipeline network and H2-Production sites. It became apparent that in the past years, the topic has gathered large momentum, leading to substantial investments in infrastructure and long-term partnerships between CO2-intensive industries and hydrogen energy providers resulting in new offerings such as Green Steel or net zero fertilizers.

Summing up, the Hydrogen Convention in Essen underpinned that the transition to hydrogen is in full swing and entering the stage in which business models are becoming economically viable, leading to a diversified and specialized ecosystem which will give SAP the opportunity to continuously support companies in becoming a net-zero player.

entry.title: Password change by BAPI_USER_CHANGE
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm creating password reset ABAP tool using BAPI_USER_UNLOCK and BAPI_USER_CHANGE in CUA environment. This ABAP tool works on central system for user unlock and set initial password for users on child system. I could find a lot of same problem in SAP community. Our system SCUM setting is correct.

Unlock incorr. login         Evrywhr

Initial password is           global

So unlocking locked user by wrong password on child system works without problem.

Alos it can set initial password to users on central system, however initial password not set to users on child system. I checked ALE log by T-CD  SCUL. I could not find corresponding ( initial password transfer to child system ) ALE log. What can I do for it ?

entry.title: Remove yellow drill down arrows in query
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to remove the yellow drill down arrows in this query. Especially for the field DocNum because the wrong documents open because of Union All.

 

 

SELECT
'Klantorder' AS 'Document',
T0.[DocDate],
T0.[CardCode],
T0.[CardName],
T0.[DocNum],
T1.[ItemCode],
T1.[Dscription],
T1.[Quantity],
T1.[TreeType]

FROM
ORDR T0
INNER JOIN RDR1 T1 ON T0.[DocEntry] = T1.[DocEntry]

WHERE
T0.[CardCode] =[%0] AND
T1.[Dscription] Like '%%[%1]%%' AND
T1.[TreeType] <> 'I' AND
T0.[CANCELED] = 'N'

UNION ALL

SELECT
'Uitgaande factuur en betaling' AS 'Document',
T0.[DocDate],
T0.[CardCode],
T0.[CardName],
T0.[DocNum],
T1.[ItemCode],
T1.[Dscription],
T1.[Quantity],
T1.[TreeType]

FROM
OINV T0
INNER JOIN INV1 T1 ON T0.[DocEntry] = T1.[DocEntry]

WHERE
T0.[CardCode] =[%0] AND
T1.[Dscription] Like '%%[%1]%%' AND
T1.[TreeType] <> 'I' AND
T0.[CANCELED] = 'N' AND
T0.[IsICT] = 'Y'

ORDER BY
2

entry.title: Re: Direct Link to SAP CRM Object BP_CONTACT
entry.content:
Hi Monika,

don't know if you figured out already, but I had the exact same problem... after debugging and trying for a while I recognized the strange SHIFTING in the URL Parameter the mapper class does to get the "two" parameters for Account and Contact Person.

Solution is you combine both GUIDs after each other into the crm-object-value parameter. Account first, Contact Person directly after.

Here is the URL I came up with that works:

https://<server>?crm-object-type=BP_CONTACT&crm-object-action=B&crm-object-keyname=CP_KEY&crm-object-value=249D0815AFDE294193829E3B481F76E7001999D696991EE39CC7213A5DA399D9

The logic it runs through is in the mapper class CL_CRM_BUPA_UIU_OBJ_MAPPER in method IF_CRM_UI_OBJ_MAPPER~GET_ENTITY_FROM_UI_OBJECT.

Kind regards
Chris

entry.title: Re: Editable virtual elements
entry.content: "Thank you for the prompt response! I would like to show my situation: Here are two fields - one regular and one virtual:"

entry.title: Automate creating the credit segment for a new business partner.
entry.content:
• Using the job template “Update Credit Limit for Business Partners” automate the process of creating the credit segment for a new business partner by using the scheduling option.
  • Request for a selection field to provide the country(ex. PO) as a selection parameter for running the job to update the credit segment for new business partners only in the provided country.
  • Use country selection parameter to schedule template job for every country and automate the credit segment creation for business partners.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Automate creating the credit segment for a new busine...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please, find the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3487288

 

Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Editable virtual elements
entry.content:
@Andre_Fischer , thank you for the prompt response! I would like to show my situation: Here are two fields - one regular and two virtual:

 This is a UI annotations :


Here is result:

Unfortunately, this is applicable only to regular fields...
Can you advise what to do in this situation?

Kind regards,
Ivan

Hi @sdig89 ,

I'm pretty much at the same point as you're. I wanna enhance the TBT116RPO app aswell.

Did you try this way:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/adding-custom-fields-in-webclient-ui-apps/ba-p/13480365

Let me know if you have further information please.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: Description not getting reflecting Change Re...
entry.content:
Hi SCN Folks, 

got the solution the only thing needs to give is - in initiator row, after initiator step member need to assign in Next Step Column, 

Thank You,

Suggu Sandeep 🙂

 

SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: Re: People Analytics - Deprecation of classic design expe...
entry.content:
Hi @lukaszkubiak ,

Thank you for posting your query and a valid question. Currently Blending in Table widget is not supported in ODE (Optimized Design Experience). We are aware of this and working towards closing this gap. Unfortunately there is no workaround for this in ODE if table widget is your requirement and you will have to continue using the Classic Design stack for now.

Best Regards

Ayon

entry.title: Re: Delivery completed indicator cannot be set for item 0...
entry.content: You cannot set that manually.  Check SAP note 1093582 which explains in detail

entry.title: Integration of custom entity to view entity or projection
entry.content:
Hello Together!
In my RAP application I defined a custom entity to display and edit standard text (read by READ_TEXT function module )

Can we use standard view entities and custom entities together, with the custom entity using implementation via a class, and in the root view entity using the standard logic for selecting from tables (for example, for activity)? If not, what is the way to integrate custom selects using a class (in my case, for LONG TEXTS) and standard tools like CDS with selects from standard tables?

I'm working on S4HANA 2023.
I have already left a comment on a similar topic (26.06.2024):
 How to implement a custom entity in the ABAP RESTf... - Page 2 - SAP Community


Kind regards, 
Ivan

entry.title: SAP Security: Moving to SU24 approach from manual authorization maintenance
entry.content:
Hi All,

Need guidance on below question which is very critical from my project point of view

Currently, we are not using SU24 approach in our project, means we don’t add tcodes in menu tab in role and adding tcodes in S_TCODE and only required authorization objects are added in role manually.

Now, we are going to convert our system into S/4 HANA and apart from this our client also want to convert roles into SU24 approach.

Thus, I wanted to know

  1. What activities need to be performed from SU25 and SU24 tcodes point of view
  2. Precautionary steps need to be performed
  3. Activities from role changes point of view

    Please share your suggestions or any documentations to perform these activities.

    Thanks

    Madhu K

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 3 - Image Embed
entry.content: I also saw that Q2 HANA Cloud is also now supporting Vector in Graph. I would have been curious about which use cases you would have chosen to illustrate that. Example, use COMMUNITIES_LOUVAIN Built-In Graph Algorithm to create communities of similar pets...

entry.title: Is there a way to distribute external learning certificates to users in Learning?
entry.content:
In our company, HR organizes all external trainings. Usually HR receives the certificates of those external trainings afterwards and then sends them to users via mail. Is there any way to distribute external learning certificates to users through LMS?

We don't want to create items for each external learning as these trainings usually don't happen repeatedly. We know that users can upload the certificates themselves, but that would require them receiving their certificate first.

Thank you for any ideas!

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Public Cloud: Product Compliance - Assign Com...
entry.content:
Dear Snigdhodeep,

I truly appreciate you taking the time to reach out to us. It's wonderful to see you designing your own compliance purposes and utilizing the supplier information pattern.

Please note that for a compliance requirement to be assigned to a compliance purpose, there must be at least one active compliance requirement version. Consequently, the dialog “Assign compliance requirements” only offers activated compliance requirements. You might want to consider activating at least one compliance requirements version of your preferred compliance requirement within the app "Activate Compliance Requirements – Product Marketability".

We acknowledge that our documentation may need enhancements in some areas. Rest assured, we are committed to making continuous improvements in this regard.

All the best with your work! Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any further questions.

Best Regards, Andrea

entry.title: S4HANA Public Cloud_Order confirmation form_change output is not triggering automatically
entry.content:
In S4HANA Public Cloud, Sales order confirmation EDI output is not triggering automatically whenever changes done to the sales order (Ex. Quantity, Dates, or other fields). 

In Output Parameter Determination app I have maintained the settings as below:- 

Output Type: ORDER CONFIRMATION CHANGE

Channel: EDI

Recipient: Sold To Number 

Could you please help me to understand what could be the issue or missing any configuration.

entry.title: Re: During Sales Order creation , need to split line items when enter is pressed.
entry.content:
Your requirement is not sure whether it is Business requirement.  Once you give the material code, quantity and hit enter key, based on what parameter, this line item should get split ?  Quantity ??  May be, it would be easy to assist you if you can explain the what the Business requirement is.  Without knowing the impact and the SAP functionality, please do not go for user exits.

Finally, split will happen in the subsequent documents like delivery and billing based on certain parameters and if you are clear on this, then, this question would not have arised I believe.

entry.title: Re: Password change by BAPI_USER_CHANGE
entry.content:
Hello,

here is a similar request with an accepted answer. What I can also imagine to solve the problem, is the RFC use of BAPI_USER_CHANGE. But I did not try it, maybe BAPI_USER_CHANGE rejects this call on a CUA client system. Should be easy to test this.

call function 'BAPI_USER_CHANGE' destination yourCUAClient
    EXPORTING USERNAME = theUser
              PASSWORD = newPassword
              PASSWORDX = 'X'
    TABLES RETURN = bapiReturn.

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Sharing Power, Doubling Success - That's Co-Leadership @ SAP! 🚀 #1 Tandem
entry.content:
Breaking New Ground: The Power Duo of Co-Leadership with Mathias and Melanie!

The Co-Leadership model has been a game changer in the landscape of HR & Leadership best practices. Following our initial blog post on Co-Leadership, we're excited to spotlight our first duo of our blog series: Melanie Gomell and Mathias Braje. Since September 2023, they have jointly led the Global Health Management Team, operating both full-time from our headquarter in Walldorf. We are delighted that both Melanie and Mathias kindly agreed to share their experiences with us. What follows is a summary of their insightful reflections. We hope you find it as enjoyable to read as we did! 

 

Why Co-Leadership?

Mathias shared that implementing the Co-Leadership model was motivated by the need for a new management layer within the organization. They recognized the need for a robust and effective management system - not just for their team, but also for the supervising manager. They found that a Co-Leadership approach was the perfect fit.

Melanie added on the advantages of the Co-Leadership model. She sees it as an opportunity to merge strengths. In their case, these merged skills include the ability to lead large-scale projects, provide systemic coaching, manage innovation, work in global organizations, and maintain availability. More importantly, Melanie stressed that shared leadership allows effective handling of ongoing projects and issues, ensuring no leadership tasks are overlooked.

 

How do you delegate work & responsibilities?

Melanie explained that responsibilities and tasks are often assigned based on the topic. At the moment, both she and Mathias handle joint meetings as many topics are interlinked. Key decisions, including those related to salaries, are made collectively.

Mathias emphasizes the importance of daily co-lead meetings, followed by team meetings. These meetings are vital in task allocation, helping them to prioritize and handle constraints.

 

How did you navigate the process of determining and dividing your responsibilities as Co-Leaders?

Melanie explained us that right from the start, they both had clear discussions about how they would share their Co-Leadership duties. She said, we trust each other's decisions entirely and make major ones together. To keep everything in order, they have a quick daily meeting. Although they concentrate on certain areas, they also have a good overall understanding. 

We thought about dividing tasks so one of us would handle all staff issues and the other would focus more on other topics. However, this didn't work for us because we are each specialized in different areas.

They thought of other possibilities of work division, e.g. one of them is responsible for all personnel topics and the other would focus more on other topics. However, this didn't work for them because they are both experts for specific topics. 

 

The Impact of Co-Leadership

Melanie shared the noticeable positive shifts within the team since implementing the Co-Leadership model. Benefits span from empowering the team and strengthening support, to establishing 'new work' structures. For example, the team conducts regular retrospectives and daily team touchpoints - ways that encourage easy queries and maximize the team's collective intelligence. 

Mathias echoes these thoughts, explaining the insightful process of running daily meetings that create “a great team spirit and hands-on mentality”. One important feature of the Co-Leadership model is the accessibility which ensures that both managers are always available to the team, and vice versa. This forsters an environment of shared support and collaboration.

 

Your Team’s Feedback on Co-Leadership

Both Melanie and Mathias were thrilled to share that the reception of the Co-Leadership model from their team has been extremely positive, according to the latest employee survey. Their team values the quicker response times and more closer interactions with their management. By bridging unique strengths, the Co-Leadership model paves the way for a deeply collaborative and supportive work environment.

 

How did you overcome challenges within your Co-Leadership?

In our survey, Mathias underlined that trust, shared values, and constant communication are the cornerstones for successfully handling challenges that come with shared leadership. In summary, both Melanie and Mathias agree that the success of the Co-Leadership model rests on having a shared vision, promoting open communication, and most importantly, embracing collaboration and trust as fundamental leadership values.

 

A huge thank you to Melanie and Mathias for sharing their time and insights with us. Stay tuned! Next week, we're catching up with Rebekka Hoferer and Simon Kessler. They're the dynamic duo leading the Customer Advisory of the BTP team in Switzerland. We're excited to share their experiences and unique lessons learned. Stay tuned! 🌟

entry.title: Highlights of Discover SAP PLM - June 2024 Edition
entry.content:
We are excited to share the highlights from our recent virtual event, Discover SAP PLM - June 2024 Edition. This event brought together a dynamic community of industry experts and SAP enthusiasts to explore the latest advancements in SAP Product Lifecycle Management (PLM).

Our distinguished speakers from SAP — Ismail Serin, Carsten Dietze-Selent, Felix Reichle, Ing-Marie Loennerheden, and Christoph Golinski and Eudes Canuto — provided invaluable insights into the achievements of our latest releases and offered an exclusive glimpse into the future roadmap of SAP PLM. Additionally, we were thrilled to feature Lars from LeverX, who shared exciting updates and initiatives from the SAP PLM community. The event featured topics that like SAP PLM’s strategic initiatives, practical applications for discrete and process industries, and innovative AI-driven advancements available in SAP PLM, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of the evolving SAP PLM landscape.

But it wasn't just about presentations; attendees actively participated in discussions, asked questions, and engaged with peers, making it a truly interactive experience. Whether you were able to join us live or are catching up now, we invite you to dive into our recap of this insightful virtual event and stay informed about the latest developments in SAP PLM through this blog post.

SAP PLM Strategy and Road Map with Ismail and Carsten

In their conversation, Ismail and Carsten discuss the strategic direction and technological advancements of SAP PLM software, particularly emphasizing their new Cloud-native solution built on the business technology platform. Ismail starts by highlighting the challenges faced by various industries in product development, including increasing complexity, regulatory requirements, and the need for deeper integration across engineering and manufacturing processes. He underscores the importance of brand new SAP PLM SaaS in addressing these challenges through its comprehensive capabilities and integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP. Carsten elaborates on the integration framework, detailing how SAP PLM integrates with various tools and systems like CAD, PDM, and SAP S/4HANA to streamline processes from design through manufacturing. They emphasize the goal of enabling seamless data flow and process synchronization to enhance product development efficiency and innovation delivery.

SAP PLM for Process Industries with Felix and Ing-Marie

In this presentation, Felix provided an in-depth look at how their PLM system handles specification management processes. He emphasized the critical role of detailed property management and versioning in ensuring compliance and maintaining quality throughout the product lifecycle. Demonstrations highlighted the system's capabilities in accessing and managing specifications through a user-friendly interface, with robust filtering and comparison tools for enhanced decision-making. The session showcased how specifications are structured and managed, with a focus on integrating with other systems like SAP S/4HANA for comprehensive traceability and operational efficiency. Future plans include expanding features, deeper integrations, and API enhancements, underscoring their commitment to advancing usability and functionality for all users involved in product development and compliance.

In contrast, Ing-Marie focused on sharing a brand-new SAP PLM capability called formulation. She demonstrated the system's capability in recipe management, highlighting structured inputs, processing instructions, and the flexibility to create and manage recipes efficiently. The session emphasized how formulation data integrates with specification management, ensuring that ingredient inputs align with specified product requirements and compliance standards. Integration with other PLM capabilities and systems like SAP S/4HANA further enhances efficiency and collaboration across teams. Future developments include expanding feature sets and enhancing integrations to support seamless product development and formulation processes, reflecting their ongoing commitment to improving usability and functionality for users across the organization.

SAP PLM for Process Industries with Gerhard Himmelsbach

Gerhard Himmelsbach introduced another brand-new capability in SAP PLM called, Product Data Integration, emphasizing its versatility across CAD systems—from on-premises deployments to private and public cloud environments. This integration seamlessly connects CAD tools such as SolidWorks, eCAD solutions, and PCB design tools with SAP PLM, ensuring comprehensive data transfer including 3D geometries and material specifications. Gerhard highlighted the integration’s role in enhancing production planning, compliance checks, and downstream processes like MRP runs. He showcased new UI enhancements within CAD applications, underscoring improved user interaction and data visibility across the product lifecycle. This session exemplified our commitment to integrating engineering and enterprise processes effectively, empowering product-centric companies to innovate sustainably.

Get to know the perspective of SAP PLM from our Community – An interview with Lars Olson from LeverX

In my conversation with Lars Olsen from LeverX, we discussed their role as a global systems integrator and SAP partner, specializing in SAP PLM solutions. Lars emphasized LeverX's extensive experience and status as a trusted gold partner of SAP, with a collaboration that spans more than two decades. We briefly touched upon LeverX's innovative solutions highlighting their commitment to enhancing efficiency during cloud transitions. Looking forward, Lars shared insights into LeverX's recent webinar series on BTP and PLM, underscoring their dedication to educating stakeholders about emerging technologies in this field. Check out their webinar series. Want to know more about LeverX? Connect with them at their next event LEVEREDGE.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP PLM with Christoph

Christoph delivered an insightful presentation on the integration of artificial intelligence within SAP PLM, underscoring its pivotal role in driving innovation and efficiency across various business domains. He elaborated on strategic initiatives aimed at leveraging AI to streamline product lifecycle management processes, emphasizing the importance of robust partnerships and cutting-edge technologies. Christoph showcased practical applications such as AI-driven innovation management and advanced recipe formulation tools like SAP Joule in SAP PLM, illustrating their transformative impact on optimizing product development cycles and enhancing marketing strategies.

Featuring e-bite called SAP PLM in the Cloud with Eudes

Eudes shared a brief update during the session, announcing the release of a new e-bite publication focused on SAP PLM in the cloud. He is one of the authors of this read and he expressed pleasure in collaborating with colleagues on this project, highlighting that the book offers comprehensive insights into SAP PLM's capabilities across approximately 100 pages. He extended gratitude to partners such as Gramont, All for One, LeverX, DSC Software AG, and Linx-AS, and thanked SAP Press for their collaboration. Purchase your copy today

As we concluded the Discover SAP PLM - June 2024 Edition, we encouraged you to revisit the insights shared during the event by accessing the replay and presentation materials. We also invited you to join the SAP Community, try out a free trial, explore the SAP Help Portal, and visit our .sap.com page for more resources and updates. Are you on LinkedIn? Join our SAP PLM Experts Forum and never miss out on real-time updates! I would like to thank my colleague Sina Goetz for an amazing LinkedIn promotion of our Virtual Event together with our speakers – let us know in the comments if this is how you got to learn about it! We extend our heartfelt thanks to our presenters for their efforts and to you, our SAP PLM community for your active participation and support.

entry.title: Unintended reinstall of SSAM app
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm encountering an issue with my SSAM application where app is reinstalling multiple times randomly. I'm just  making changes on metadata and launching

Issue: Chokidar is raising a change event for a file that hasn't been manually modified, which is causing the app to be reinstalled repeatedly. This is the log message I get from VS Code:

 

Details:

  • This behavior started appearing about two weeks ago after macOS upgrade.
  • I am using the latest version of VS Code.
  • I am using MDK 23.8.4

Any insights, suggestions, or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

Abuzar Baig Mirza

entry.title: Re: Controlling the Visibility of SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion Content
entry.content:
Hello Shane,

Kudos to you for such a helpful blog for in-App users. On a side note, do you maybe have insights whether the public audience (without an S-user account) could get access to the published content?

This would be in the scenarios where I want to show potential customers what SEN could provide without them having to have an account!

Thanks a lot in advance for your support! 

Best,

Anh Nguyen

entry.title: Re: Password change by BAPI_USER_CHANGE
entry.content:
Hello,

Can I specify target system in parameter of BAPI_USER_CHANGE ? If I test this BAPI I can see box which I can specify target system target system. Named  in 'RFC target sys:'. Which parameter can I set target system ?

I attach our SCUM setting.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan Call Dates called earlier than Call...
entry.content: Greetings, how often do you schedule the maintenance plan - do you run IP30 daily? It is possible that the cycle is reached before 45 days. Perhaps also think why do you use call horizon - would it be okay if the maintenance call object would be created when the cycle reaches 1000?

entry.title: Funds Management Accounting transaction is not considering Budget Amount at all
entry.content:
Hi All

I have configured Funds Management in S4Hana. When I post an accounting entry in F-02 in the system it is not picking up budget amount against the GL. No error or warning message is issued.

Accounting document as well as Controlling document are posted but no FM document is posted. 

I have verified my configuration and data derivation in FMDerive  as well.

Please let me know how to resolve the issue.

Rajeev Uppal

entry.title: Re: Setting up the COPAA Archieve Object
entry.content:
Hi,

OK, so you have to create the operating concern according your business rules. You can doit by copying an SAP operating concern template by customizing:

SPRO > Controlling > Profitability Analysis > Structures > Define Operating Concern > Sample Operating Concerns > 

then run the 2 sub-entries:

> Use SAP Operating Concern Template

> Copy Operating Concern including Customizing

Further information in below link:
Archiving of CO-PA Transaction Data | SAP Help Portal

Regards

Joseph

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan Call Dates called earlier than Call...
entry.content: Yes, IP30 is run daily via background job. We are using call horizon for MRP consideration. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Last Invoice Date in List of Customer Business Partners
entry.content:
Hi @kedalenechong ,

There are multiple ways to do this, a simple one would be assigning an FMS to an existing or custom field, triggering it from the field CardCode.

A VERY basic one:

SELECT
    MAX(RefDate)
FROM JDT1
WHERE 1 = 1
    AND ShortName = $[$5.0.0]
    AND TransType IN ('13', '14') -- Here you define which Objects are being looked into. In this case, the query is searching for AR Invoices and Credits, respectively.

Of course, the query above will bring the last document, which might be either an Invoice or a Credit. You may just adjust it to bring the document type you need, taking into consideration the differences between customers and suppliers. Also, it's not considering whether the document is cancelled or not, so, if you need to take it into account, it's a different story. This is just something to give you an idea on how this could be done and play around.

Apart from this, you could use a Stored Procedure to update data in a UDF or even use Add-Ons such as B1UP to do the job. Again, multiple ways.

I hope it helps,

Felipe

entry.title: Re: Architecting Complex Real-Time Business Solutions: SAP Build Process Automation with Auto Approv
entry.content: Thanks a lot @Murali_Shan for your kinds words. Here is the next blog of our series https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/architecting-complex-real-time-business-solutions-sap-bpa-with-dynamic/ba-p/13739684

entry.title: Re: Need to schedule a maintenance order on particularly...
entry.content: Greetings, If actual dates are not so important you can create a maintenance plan with cycle 15 days and setup start date as 6th of next month. The date will vary from month to month unfortunately. But if the date is required then I think you need to develop some additional functionalities with help of developers.

entry.title: Re: Architecting Complex Real-Time Business Solutions: SAP Build Process Automation with Auto Approv
entry.content: Thanks @Ravi_MDU good to hear about it. Please feel free in case needs any clarifications for the same.

entry.title: How do you name your global classes ?
entry.content:
Hi !

Maybe it's a dumb question... But I'm always wondered how others developers named their global classes, because our namespace (Z & Y) is pretty limited since everything created in the repository can be created once (for the same type). I would loved a solution where the namespace is linked to a package and so it will be possible to name 2 repository objects identically into 2 separate package, sweet dream.

Because of this, I often find my self using local classes in my programs, I find them more flexible and I can avoid to pollute unnecessarily the repository with odd names. But there are costs, one of them is reusability. Of course, I create global classe when it's make sense, like business object that I know there will be only one classe like ZCL_EQUIPEMENT or ZCL_DELIVERY (with some exceptions to the rule of course) or classes with clear boundaries.

But what about classes like MODEL / DAO / VIEW / CONTROLLER and so on ? If we are using MVC pattern in our development, we have to deal with this classes in multiples reports, so if global, name conflict may happen when for example 2 MODEL in different applications are created with slightly different interface but with the same semantic.

What are your recommandation about it ?

Thanks !

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 4 - Geospatial
entry.content: India to AIRBUS HQ .......................

entry.title: Automatic serial number determination during confirmation of production order in S/4HC ?
entry.content:
Hello, 

How automatic serial number can be generate during confirmation production order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition . 

We having option serial number determination during creation and release of production order  but as in mentioned above we are looking at confirmation of production.

Thank  in advance . 
Harvind Narayan

entry.title: Outbound delivery assignment to TU in EWM / TM Integration.
entry.content:
Hi ,

I have a basic question on TM / EWM integration in outbound process to end customer.

We do planning in TM and we get a TU in EWM, durring loading if user by accident select the TU which is not the actual one by mistake, is there any check in standard to restrict user to use only the TUs that are valid for that specific delivery?

entry.title: Re: How and where do you start a consulting career in SAP?
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

 

First of all, I would like to thank and appreciate you for your post about the digital skills initiative.

This will help people to learn about SAP from scratch and push the boundaries of those who are part of the SAP Journey from different backgrounds of life especially for women who want to return to the work. I worked as Senior SAP Consultant and looking forward to learn more specifically SAP Integration and Finance and SAP BTP is a great resource to work on end to end integration across the systems.

Best Regards,

Harini DT.

entry.title: Re: Direct Link to SAP CRM Object BP_CONTACT
entry.content:
Hi Chris

Thank you for your reply.

I am not sending GUID but instead am sending CONP_NUMBER from UI. If user click on a contact ID i.e. CONP_NUMBER it should open the details with the account with which it has Main Relationship with. This works perfectly fine if contact id is linked to only one Account

but gives problem if contact id is linked to multiple account ids. I raised a ticket with SAP Product team as well -They told me that standard code is not equipped to handled two URL parameters Objects needs to be customized to accommodate this.

 

Thanks

Monika

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 4 - Geospatial
entry.content: Done with my week 4 submission .I experimented with Atlanta to France route.It was very good challenge SAP Hana Multi Model.Got learn many new Things.  @Vitaliy-R  Thanks.

entry.title: SAP CAP: different names for "EntityType" and "EntitySet"
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm working on consuming an existing on-premise OData service using CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model). The original service has an EntitySet called "AssetSet" with elements of type "Asset". The metadata looks something like this:

<EntitySet Name="AssetSet" EntityType="API.Asset"/>

I've imported the external service and defined in CAP as follows:

using { API } from './external/api';

extend service mirror_api {

  entity Asset as projection on API.Asset;

}

This produces metadata that looks like:

<EntitySet Name="Asset" EntityType="mirror_api.Asset"/>

My goal is to maintain the original EntitySet name ("AssetSet") instead of the current output ("Asset"). The metadata would look just like it is in the on-premise system, with an “EntitySet” named “AssetSet” and an “EntityType” named “API.Asset”. Is there a way to achieve this within CDS?

I've searched the documentation but haven't found a clear solution. I know that this was not supported back in 2019 (when someone else asked about it here).

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Best regards,

Rafael

entry.title: Excel customer contact / BP information from CRM to Excel
entry.content:
Can anyone advise on the best way to extract customer contact / BP information from CRM into Excel?

We are changing systems and need to extract the CRM information for migration via excel. How best to do this? What tables would I need to use to gain this information, is it via SE16?

Also, does anyone know the tables used to export contact information, e.g. notes / contact IDs / etc.

Thanks

entry.title: Is it possible to add another column/extension field to the open tasks view within my launchpad ?
entry.content:
Is it possible to add another column/extension field to the open tasks in work view of Home workcenter ? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add another column/extension field...
entry.content:
Hi

It is not possible to add extension fields via adaptation or personalization mode to Home work center in open tasks view.

entry.title: Message size limit in sap cloud integration
entry.content:
Hi,

There is always a debate between cloud integration vs ETL tool and when to use what especially when an org has both but managed by different teams.

One of the important factor is message size. However I have not come across any blog or piece of information that mentions the message size limit in cloud integration for each transaction. Can someone share the same?

Also if there are some points of considerations to make for CI vs ETL

@finny_babu @Sriprasadsbhat 

Best Regards

entry.title: P2P controlling analysis in Sap Process Insights
entry.content:
Hello Community.
Is it possible to analyze process flow related to Procure to Pay process with controlling data details (cost center, internal order, WBS). It seems that controlling data are not managed in details for PR,PO, invoice.

Thanks in advance.

Giuseppe

entry.title: Performance Status: Rejected
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I oversee P&G and recently reviewed some Performance Plans where I noticed a status marked as "rejected."

I was wondering, as employees should not have the ability to reject a Performance Plan. Could you explain how the system could generate this action?

Regards

Firi

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Public Cloud: Product Compliance - Assign Com...
entry.content:
Hi Snigdhodeep,

Sorry for the confusion with the relationship between compliance purposes and compliance requirement. Here is one example on the help portal where it shows you need to activate a compliance requirement before assigning it to a compliance purpose. I think your issue highlights the need for us to have this point in the in-app help. Thank you for sharing this problem. Best wishes, Michael

entry.title: Re: Sap S4Hana Cloud - Logistics Incoming Invoice - Accounting document type determination
entry.content:
Hi Alessandra, are you aware of some updates in the S4H Public cloud about this feature?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Article Master Update - LSMW /BDC / BAPI ?
entry.content: Hey , im having same scenario. if you have any BAPI can you share please

entry.title: SAP Build WorkZone MyInbox App - Local copy
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I would like to create a tile in Work Zone standard of My Inbox app with showAdditionalAttributes = true.

This requires me to create a local copy of My Inbox Html5 app.

Unfortunately, after that the Count at the tile stops working.

In the browser console I can see that there is Internal server error 500.

Do you have any ideas what could be the problem?

Thank you,

Patrik

entry.title: SAP BW/4HANA Data Model for SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting
entry.content:
Co-Authored: Andreas Tenholte and Philip Fengler

Introduction

We in the CoE EMEA Team for Data Warehousing & Data Management have developed a Group Reporting data model for SAP BW/4HANA. This model is now out of the pilot phase and is already being used successfully by several customers.

In the following, we would like to give you a brief overview of the model's target audience, its structure and functionality, as well as information on how to get access to the model if you are interested. So stay tuned, it's worth reading 😊

 

Target Group for the SAP BW/4HANA Group Reporting Model

A data model for SAP BW/4HANA has been requested by several customers who have one or more of the following requirements:

  • Use of SAP BW/4HANA as the central reporting platform for the execution of financial consolidation reporting.
  • The need to combine group reporting data with analytical data such as HCM KPIs, production data, etc.
  • Use the flexible analysis authorization framework to fine-tune row-level access based on hierarchy nodes such as consolidation unit and profit center.
  • The need to load and distribute group reporting data to other systems.

 

Interrelation between SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting and the BW Group Reporting Model

The provided SAP BW/4HANA data model is implemented based on the data structures used in SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting and leverages standard CDS views delivered as part of SAP S/4HANA, to extract and load the data into the receiving data model in SAP BW/4HANA. This data is then made available for reporting by applying group reporting logic including hierarchy elimination logic.

 

About the SAP BW/4HANA Group Reporting Model

The SAP BW/4HANA data model consists of Z*-objects (BW objects, ABAP CDS Views, HANA Calculation Views), covering transactional and master data. The model is aligned with SAP best practices, such as “Layered-Scalable-Architecture optimized for HANA” (LSA++) and HANA-optimized business content. The BW data model is currently not part of the official SAP BW/4 Business Content. This aspect requires further elaboration.

The following Group Reporting functionality is covered by the data model:

  • Version relations including extension versions
  • Group derivation logic based on the group structure
  • Hierarchy elimination
  • Period mode

The reports that can be created using the SAP BW/4HANA data model for Group Reporting provide the same functionality as the SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics reports of Group Data Analysis.

It is also possible to extend the functional scope of the Group Reporting data model to meet individual business requirements as part of the service delivery (see below):

  • Downporting the SAP BW/4HANA data model to SAP BW on HANA 7.50
  • Reporting rules to cover Cash Flow reporting, that corresponds to Group Data Analysis including Reporting Rules in SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics
  • Handling of physical deletions

 

How to get access to the SAP BW/4HANA Group Reporting Model?

The service delivery is carried out by the CoE EMEA Team for Data Warehousing and Data Management as part of the Premium Engagement. 

Below is an overview of the service delivery plan and the added value of the collaboration as part of the Premium Engagement.

Service Plan Proposal

  • Enablement Workshop for Data and Analytics is the service to get to understand the SAP BW/4HANA pilot data model and, based on customer-specific business demands, identify potential gaps resulting in a high-level design and project plan for the proof-of-concept phase (Jump Start Service).
  • Jump-start for Analytics and Data Management to deploy the pilot data model for SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting in customers SAP BW/4HANA sandbox system with the adjustments as scoped, testing, final sign-off including hand-over to the project team.
  • Safeguarding Services, on Performance Optimization (BPPO, TPO), Quality Assurance Assessments (FTC, TIC), Going Live Support, etc. complete the service plan proposal.

Value

  • Speed up Time-to-Value: Fast deployment of a proven pilot data model for SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting with SAP BW/4HANA.
  • Know-how-transfer how the data model works including key design considerations.
  • Utilize the know-how of SAP experts to reduce the TCO of the application model for Group Reporting in SAP BW/4HANA e.g., by understanding Data Tiering Optimization options.
  • Option to extend and enrich the model based on customers specific requirements for Group Reporting by the input and help of SAP experts.

If you are interested in getting in touch with us, you will find the necessary contacts in our Focus Package.

entry.title: Re: OMR4 - How change document type in MIRO
entry.content: Hi, are you aware of some updates in the S4H Public cloud about a feature similar to the OMR4 tcode? Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Change in Reference number - After RERAPP
entry.content: Thanks its correct.

entry.title: Re: Error in Scrap Sales Order
entry.content: The error message is self-explanatory.  You need to extend the material to the required Sales Area without which, you cannot create sale order for that material.
